The '''perfect''' in Aelaki refers to completed action, expressed through two distinct sub-aspects that differ in telicity: the [[Atelic|atelic perfect]] and the [[Telic|telic perfect]].

== Overview ==

Aelaki distinguishes between two types of completion based on whether the action had an inherent endpoint (telic) or not (atelic). Both are formed through stem modification of the base verb form (C₁V₁C₂V₂C₃).

{| class="wikitable"
! Aspect !! Formation !! Example !! Meaning
|-
| [[Atelic|Atelic perfect]] || o-grade vowels || zhoron || is alive (state achieved, no endpoint)
|-
| [[Telic|Telic perfect]] || umlaut (vowel fronting) || zheren || lived (life completed, goal reached)
|}

All examples use the root ''zh-r-n'' "live" (base form: ''zharan'').

== Atelic perfect ==

The atelic perfect expresses the present result of an unbounded action. It indicates that something has happened but without implying that a goal was reached or that the action is definitively over. In the '''present tense''', the atelic perfect takes on a '''[[Habitual|habitual]]''' meaning.

== Telic perfect ==

The telic perfect expresses a completed action whose inherent goal or natural endpoint has been achieved. In the '''present tense''', the telic perfect takes on a '''[[Gnomic|gnomic]]''' meaning, expressing universal truths.

== Present tense mappings ==

{| class="wikitable"
! Past aspect !! Present realization
|-
| Atelic perfect || [[Habitual]]
|-
| Telic perfect || [[Gnomic]]
|}

== Contrast with imperfect ==

The perfect aspects contrast with the [[Imperfect|imperfective]] ([[Continuous|continuative]]) aspect, which expresses ongoing action rather than completed action.

{| class="wikitable"
! Category !! Aspect !! Example !! Meaning
|-
| [[Imperfect|Imperfective]] || [[Continuous|Continuative]] || zhororon || living (in progress)
|-
| Perfective (atelic) || Atelic perfect || zhoron || is alive (state achieved)
|-
| Perfective (telic) || Telic perfect || zheren || lived (life completed)
|}

== See also ==

* [[Atelic]]
* [[Telic]]
* [[Imperfect]]
* [[Habitual]]
* [[Gnomic]]

[[Category:grammatical tenses]]
[[Category:git synced pages]]
